sections.title: The Decision Room
sections.subhead: $39 per month or $390 per year (2 months free)
body: A private room for Black founders building at every level.
If you are an established CEO who wants sharper insight and serious Black operators around you, this is for you.
If you run a growing business and want better tools and better judgment around your next move, this is for you.
If you're early and shaping your idea, this is for you.
This is where Black ownership gets sharper.

image.alt: Amanda Johnson in a white studio portrait.

sections.title: Inside The Decision Room you get:
body: Community. Structure. Access.
list: Live Working Sessions
A Private Founder Channel
Priority Access to Blind Dinners
A growing Decision Tool Library
A clear pathway to one-on-one Business Counsel

image.alt: Portrait layered over a geometric backdrop.

sections.title: Live Working Sessions
body: Members-only sessions where real business decisions get worked through in real time.
Not panels. Not lectures.
list: Founders bring real questions.
We walk through the tradeoffs.
We pressure-test the options.
You see how serious decisions are framed and made.
bodyAfter: If you ever want that level of focus on your situation alone, that's what Business Counsel is for.

image.alt: Portrait framed by soft light and layered textiles.

sections.title: Private Founder Channel
body: A private space for Black founders inside the room.
Small. Intentional. Serious.
list: Quick questions
Sanity checks
Honest conversations
bodyAfter: This is your bench when you don't have one.

image.alt: Portrait of a founder in conversation.

sections.title: Priority Access to Blind Dinners
body: Members receive priority consideration for future Blind Dinners.
The room doesn't only live online.

image.alt: Guests gathered around a dinner table.

sections.title: This is where structure lives.
body: These tools are built to help you work through your own numbers, your own offers, and your own decisions.

sections.title: Pricing Reality Check
body: Run the math before fear runs you.
If you sell shampoo and conditioner sets for $28 and raise them to $32, what actually happens if you lose a few customers?
Most founders never calculate it.
Inside the full worksheet, you'll walk through:
list: What happens to your revenue if you raise your price
How many sales you could lose and still make more money
Whether hesitation is about the market or about fear
How to communicate a price shift calmly

image.alt: Amanda Johnson seated in a studio portrait.

sections.title: Should I Launch This? Checklist
body: Excitement is not a launch plan.
Before adding a new service, product, or offering, this checklist helps you slow down and ask:
list: Will this make money
Do you have time to support it
Is your offer clear
What are you giving up by launching now
bodyAfter: Most failed launches skipped this step.

sections.title: Vendor & Partnership Checklist
body: Before signing anything, pause.
Maybe a retailer wants to carry your product.
Maybe an influencer wants a revenue split.
Maybe a manufacturer offers better pricing.
This tool helps you think through:
list: Who controls pricing and promotion
How money is actually split
Who owns the customer relationship
What happens if things don't go as planned
bodyAfter: One bad agreement can stall a business.

sections.title: Capital Fit & Funding Decisions
body: Access to capital changes everything.
But not all money is good money.
Inside this section you'll find:
list: A Capital Fit Checklist to decide if you even need outside funding
A Funding Readiness Worksheet before you apply
A curated list of grants and funding programs for Black founders
Clear notes on who each opportunity is actually for
Real breakdowns of what debt and equity truly cost
bodyAfter: This is not about chasing money.
It's about understanding what money will require from you.

image.alt: Detailed portrait with layered color and texture.

sections.title: AI: When to Use It. When Not To.
body: AI can save time.
AI can also cost you money if you trust it blindly.
Inside you'll get:
list: Copy-and-use prompts
Ways to double-check AI responses
Clear examples of when AI helps
Clear examples of when you still need judgment
bodyAfter: AI should support your thinking, not replace it.

image.alt: Editorial portrait with bold pattern and color.

sections.title: Real Life Decisions
body: We break down real founder situations step by step.
Not highlight reels. Not theory.
Real decisions with real stakes.
For example:
list: A founder raising prices and risking customer loss
A CEO evaluating a partnership that requires giving up control
A service provider walking away from a draining client
A hairdresser deciding whether to open her own salon
A beauty brand choosing whether to accept a large retail order
bodyAfter: You see how decisions are made.

sections.title: When You Want One-on-One Time
body: Membership gives you structure and community.
Sometimes you want focused time.
Business Counsel is available when you want one decision examined closely.
list: One session
One real problem
Your numbers
Your context

sections.title: Join The Decision Room
body: If you're serious about building something that lasts, come inside.
$39 per month or $390 per year (2 months free)
